cases.passage: The Indian government has implemented a stimulus package to boost economic growth in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic. As part of this package, the government has increased public expenditure on infrastructure development, increased subsidies for farmers, and introduced a job guarantee scheme. The RBI has also cut down the policy repo rate to 4% to encourage borrowing and spending. With this stimulus package, the aggregate demand in the economy is expected to increase, which will lead to an increase in GDP. However, the increase in aggregate demand may lead to inflation if not managed properly. The government will need to balance the trade-off between growth and inflation to ensure sustainable economic development.

questions.q: Why has the Indian government implemented a stimulus package?
questions.explanation: The government has implemented a stimulus package to boost economic growth in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.

questions.q: What is the expected outcome of the stimulus package on GDP?
questions.explanation: The increase in aggregate demand is expected to lead to an increase in GDP.

questions.q: What is a potential risk associated with the increase in aggregate demand?
questions.explanation: The increase in aggregate demand may lead to inflation if not managed properly.

questions.q: What is the role of the RBI in the stimulus package?
opts: (B) To cut down the policy repo rate
(D) To introduce a job guarantee scheme
questions.explanation: The RBI has cut down the policy repo rate to 4% to encourage borrowing and spending.

questions.q: What is the trade-off that the government needs to balance?
questions.explanation: The government needs to balance the trade-off between growth and inflation to ensure sustainable economic development.

cases.passage: The Indian government has introduced a new tax policy to increase tax revenue and reduce the budget deficit. As part of this policy, the government has increased the income tax rates for high-income individuals and reduced the corporate tax rates for small and medium-sized enterprises. The government has also introduced a new tax on luxury goods to reduce consumption. With this new tax policy, the government expects to increase tax revenue and reduce the budget deficit. However, the increase in tax rates may lead to a decrease in aggregate demand, which could negatively impact economic growth.

questions.q: Why has the government introduced a new tax policy?
opts: (B) To increase tax revenue and reduce the budget deficit
(C) To reduce tax on luxury goods
questions.explanation: The government has introduced a new tax policy to increase tax revenue and reduce the budget deficit.

questions.q: What is the expected outcome of the new tax policy on tax revenue?
questions.explanation: The government expects to increase tax revenue with the new tax policy.

questions.q: What is a potential risk associated with the increase in tax rates?
questions.explanation: The increase in tax rates may lead to a decrease in aggregate demand, which could negatively impact economic growth.

questions.q: What type of businesses are exempt from the increase in corporate tax rates?
questions.explanation: The corporate tax rates have been reduced for small and medium-sized enterprises.

questions.q: What is the purpose of introducing a new tax on luxury goods?
questions.explanation: The government has introduced a new tax on luxury goods to reduce consumption.
